message:
fix(sync): correct notify ordering, pool fairness, rwlock starvation, and deadline safety

Notify (notify.rs):
- Reorder poll_init fast paths to check broadcast generation BEFORE
  consuming stored notification tokens. Previously, a waiter created
  before a broadcast would consume a notify_one token via the stored
  notification path, even though the broadcast generation bump alone
  should have satisfied it. This caused lost wakeups: a subsequent
  waiter that should have received the notify_one token found it
  already consumed. The fix ensures broadcast-era waiters prefer the
  generation check, leaving stored tokens for post-broadcast waiters.
- Apply the same reordering under the waiter lock to close the race
  window between the lock-free check and the locked re-check.

Pool (pool.rs):
- Add FIFO fairness enforcement to try_acquire_idle and
  should_create_resource. Previously, a late-arriving waiter could
  steal an idle resource or trigger creation ahead of earlier waiters
  in the queue. Now both paths check is_next_in_line before proceeding,
  ensuring waiters are served in registration order.

RwLock (rwlock.rs):
- Add writer_waiters > 0 check to try_write, preventing a try_write
  caller from jumping ahead of queued writers. This closes a subtle
  writer starvation window where repeated try_write calls could
  indefinitely postpone pending write waiters.

Deadline Monitor (deadline_monitor.rs):
- Replace four .unwrap() calls on Instant::checked_sub with
  unwrap_or_else(Instant::now) fallback. On platforms where the
  monotonic clock epoch is recent, subtracting 5–30 seconds from
  Instant::now() can return None, causing test panics.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
